body.value: One of our challenges was scheduling these interviews, and community members did not have a lot of time to spare. There were multiple reasons for this. As some of these knowledge practices are directly linked to their livelihood, asking for their time was initially difficult. There were cultural barriers and trust issues in certain locations where we engaged with different communities. Making them understand the technological aspects of the project is also challenging as most of the women (not all) in the communities are less educated or not educated at all. The funds for setting up a technological network initially caused this fracture between what had to be directly revealed to the community and what steps needed to come before that. The technicalities, without context, seemed alien to the age-old practices that we were trying to introduce them into.
